new_items.title: प्रधानमंत्री ने दिल्ली के शाहदरा जिले में आग लगने की घटना में हुई जनहानि पर शोक व्यक्त किया
प्रधानमंत्री ने मिशन दृष्टि के सफल प्रक्षेपण पर गैलेक्सआई को बधाई दी
प्रधानमंत्री श्री नरेन्द्र मोदी ने डॉ. आर. बालासुब्रमण्यम जी और डॉ. जोरम अनिया जी को नीति आयोग के पूर्णकालिक सदस्य नियुक्त किए जाने पर बधाई दी
रक्षा मंत्री प्रयागराज में भारतीय सेना और एसआईडीएम द्वारा संयुक्त रूप से आयोजित 3 दिवसीय नॉर्थ टेक संगोष्ठी का उद्घाटन करेंगे
भारत–कंबोडिया द्विपक्षीय सैन्य अभ्यास ‘सिनबैक्स-द्वितीय 2026’ के लिए भारतीय सेना का दल रवाना
श्री धर्मेंद्र प्रधान ने एनटीए मुख्यालय के दौरे के दौरान नीट यूजी 2026 की व्यवस्थाओं का जायजा लिया
आधार के “नए स्वरूप” से संबंधित कुछ समाचार रिपोर्टों पर स्पष्टीकरण
केंद्रीय स्वास्थ्य एवं परिवार कल्याण मंत्रालय 4 से 10 मई 2026 तक संपूर्ण भारत में ‘अग्नि सुरक्षा सप्ताह’ मनाएगा
राष्ट्रीय स्तर पर आयोजित सर्वोत्तम पद्धतियों के शिखर सम्मेलन में केंद्रीय स्वास्थ्य मंत्रालय ने बच्चों में मधुमेह मेलिटस पर व्यापक मार्गदर्शन दस्तावेज जारी किया
केंद्रीय स्वास्थ्य मंत्रालय ने सर्वोत्तम कार्यप्रणालियों पर राष्ट्रीय शिखर सम्मेलन में राष्ट्रीय बाल स्वास्थ्य कार्यक्रम (आरबीएसके) 2.0 दिशानिर्देश जारी किए
केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री श्री अमित शाह जी ने 'मिशन दृष्टि' के सफल निर्माण पर GalaxEye के वैज्ञानिकों और इंजीनियरों को हार्दिक बधाई दी
‘‘श्रमिक राष्ट्र के विश्वकर्मा हैं’’: डॉ. मनसुख मांडविया ने राष्ट्र-निर्माण में श्रमिकों की अहम भूमिका पर जोर दिया
नवीन और नवीकरणीय ऊर्जा मंत्रालय ने अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सूर्य दिवस पर ‘रन फॉर सन’ मैराथन का आयोजन किया
पंचायत विकास योजनाओं (2026-27) की तैयारी पर दो दिवसीय राष्ट्रीय कार्यशाला नई दिल्ली में आयोजित की जाएगी
पश्चिम एशिया के घटनाक्रम के आलोक में प्रमुख क्षेत्रों से संबंधित ताजा जानकारियां
Bogus Purchase Addition Remanded Due to Pending HC Decision on Similar Issue
new_items.summary:
The issue involved additions based on alleged bogus purchases without VAT payment. The Tribunal remanded the matter pending the High Court’s decision on similar cases. 

The post Bogus Purchase Addition Remanded Due to Pending HC Decision on Similar Issue appeared first on TaxGuru.

new_items.title: Section 194-IA TDS on Full Property Value Mandatory, Not Linked to Payment Timing: ITAT Bangalore
new_items.summary:
The Tribunal upheld TDS liability where tax was deducted only on part of the property value. It ruled that TDS must be applied on the entire consideration for immovable property transactions.

The post Section 194-IA TDS on Full Property Value Mandatory, Not Linked to Payment Timing: ITAT Bangalore appeared first on TaxGuru.

new_items.title: 80G Approval Allowed as No Profit Motive Found in Micro-Credit Activities: ITAT Chennai
new_items.summary:
The Tribunal held that activities linked to micro-credit and welfare schemes do not negate charitable status in absence of profit motive. The ruling directs grant of 80G approval based on genuine charitable objectives.

The post 80G Approval Allowed as No Profit Motive Found in Micro-Credit Activities: ITAT Chennai appeared first on TaxGur

new_items.title: ITAT Rebukes CIT(A) for Ignoring Tribunal Order, Directs Fresh Adjudication
new_items.summary:
Tribunal held that non-compliance with earlier appellate directions requires fresh adjudication. Key takeaway: appellate authorities must follow binding instructions.

The post ITAT Rebukes CIT(A) for Ignoring Tribunal Order, Directs Fresh Adjudication appeared first on TaxGuru.

new_items.title: Delay in Filing Form 10 Cannot Alone Defeat Section 11(2) Exemption Claim: ITAT Visakhapatnam
new_items.summary:
The Tribunal ruled that exemption under Section 11(2) should not be denied solely due to delayed filing of Form-10. It directed reconsideration where the form was available during assessment.

The post Delay in Filing Form 10 Cannot Alone Defeat Section 11(2) Exemption Claim: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru.

new_items.title: SC Upholds Quashing of Reassessment Notices Due to Unproven PE Allegations
new_items.summary:
The Supreme Court dismissed SLPs and upheld the High Court’s finding that reassessment notices lacked tangible material. The ruling reinforces that mere survey findings cannot justify reopening of assessments.

The post SC Upholds Quashing of Reassessment Notices Due to Unproven PE Allegations appeared first on TaxGuru

new_items.title: Calcutta HC Dismisses Tax Appeal Below ₹2 Crore Limit for Failure to Show CBDT Exception Applies
new_items.summary:
Court found that the appellant failed to clearly identify which exceptional clause applied. Since the tax effect was below Rs.2 crores and no valid exception was demonstrated, the Court held that there was no reason to entertain the appeal. Accordingly, the appeal and the connected application were dismissed.

The post Calcutta HC Dismisses Tax Appeal Below

new_items.title: ITAT Remands Appeal for Fresh Hearing, Imposes ₹20,000 Cost
new_items.summary:
The Tribunal set aside orders where the assessee failed to appear and granted another opportunity. It emphasized adherence to natural justice while imposing a cost.

The post ITAT Remands Appeal for Fresh Hearing, Imposes ₹20,000 Cost appeared first on TaxGuru.

new_items.title: Reopening of Assessment Upheld Due to Form 26AS Showing Undisclosed Contract Income
new_items.summary:
The Tribunal upheld reopening under Section 147 as Form 26AS reflected substantial contract receipts despite no return being filed. It ruled that such information constitutes valid grounds for belief of income escaping assessment.

The post Reopening of Assessment Upheld Due to Form 26AS Showing Undisclosed Contract Income appeared first on

new_items.title: SC Dismisses Revenue SLP Due to Delay, Upholds Depreciation on Leased Assets
new_items.summary:
The Supreme Court rejected the appeal due to unexplained delay, leaving the High Court ruling intact. The decision confirms that depreciation is allowable where leased assets are used in business.

The post SC Dismisses Revenue SLP Due to Delay, Upholds Depreciation on Leased Assets appeared first on TaxGuru.

new_items.title: Bombay HC allowed Depreciation on Leased Assets as Business Use Requirement Satisfied
new_items.summary:
The court held that leased assets qualify for depreciation since they are used in the course of business. It clarified that physical use by the assessee is not required if leasing generates business income.

The post Bombay HC allowed Depreciation on Leased Assets as Business Use Requirement Satisfied appeared first on TaxGuru.
